---
title: "What Affects Well-Being?"
subtitle: "An Exploratory Data Analysis Project"
author: "Vladyslav Rudenko"
date: last-modified
---

## About This Project

This project investigates which lifestyle, sleep, and work-related factors
are statistically associated with human well-being, stress, and productivity.
Five independently collected datasets are analysed using **Pearson correlation**
and **Principal Component Analysis (PCA)**.

Rather than merging all data into one table (which would introduce misleading
cross-population comparisons), each dataset is examined separately and the
results are compared at the end to surface recurring patterns.

---

## Quick Navigation

| Page | What you will find |
|---|---|
| [Analysis](analysis.qmd) | Full statistical report with tables, figures, equations, and citations |
| [Dashboard](dashboard.qmd) | Interactive overview — key metrics at a glance |
| [Presentation](prezentation.qmd) | Slide deck summarising findings |

---

## Datasets

Five cleaned datasets are used (see [Analysis §Datasets](analysis.qmd#sec-datasets) for details):

- **Lifestyle & Wellness** — screen time, sleep, exercise → `wellness`
- **Productivity & Habits** — daily habits, sleep, stress → `productivity`
- **Sleep & Health** — clinical sleep study → `stress_level`
- **Student Lifestyle** — study load, GPA, activity → `stress_numeric`
- **Remote Work** — work conditions, burnout → `productivity_score`

---

## Methods

Two complementary methods are applied to every dataset:

1. **Pearson correlation** — identifies variables with the strongest direct
   linear relationship with the target variable.
2. **PCA** — reveals the dominant directions of variance and which original
   variables drive them.

Results do not imply causation; they describe statistical associations
in the available samples.

---

## Source Code & Data

All code, cleaned data, and rendered output are available on GitHub:
[github.com/caxarr/mood-analisis](https://github.com/caxarr/mood-analisis)
